claim_statement: In Romans 3:24, the verb δικαιόω ("dikaioumenoi" / "being justified") is used primarily in a forensic, declarative sense (God's legal declaration of righteousness), rather than in a primarily transformative or intrinsic-renewal sense.
resolution_summary: Partial agreement on the lexical possibility of a forensic reading in this verse under historical-grammatical exegesis; sustained disagreement on whether that reading is primary or exhaustive. Divergence localizes to authority_model, interpretive_method, and clarity_hierarchy priors. No side concedes the full claim.

root.summary: All profiles agree that δικαιόω carries declarative force in Rom 3:24 within its immediate Pauline context (δικαιοσύνη θεοῦ manifested apart from works of the law). Profiles diverge on whether this forensic sense precludes or is completed by transformative participation (theosis / iustificatio as making righteous).

depends_on.notes: Immediate co-text (Rom 3:21-28) and broader Pauline usage (Rom 4-5, Gal 2-3).

divergence_point.label: Forensic primacy vs. transformative integration
divergence_point.description: Reformed profile treats forensic declaration as the controlling sense; Catholic and Orthodox profiles read it within a broader salvific economy that necessarily includes renewal.

blocking_prior.description: Whether historical-grammatical exegesis of Paul is permitted to stand alone or must be normed by patristic synthesis and rule of faith.

supporting_evidence.text_quoted: δικαιούμενοι δωρεὰν τῇ αὐτοῦ χάριτι διὰ τῆς ἀπολυτρώσεως τῆς ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ
Justification is the acceptance by which God regards us as righteous...

counter_evidence.text_quoted: Justification is not only the remission of sins, but also the sanctification and renewal of the inward man.
Theosis is the ultimate end of the economy of salvation; justification cannot be isolated from deification.

children.summary: Red-team review: early partial lexical agreement flagged as suspicious; patristic usage of dikaio- vocabulary (e.g., in Athanasius, Cyril) consistently blends forensic and transformative senses. Challenge: the tree must explain why Paul’s forensic emphasis in this pericope does not control the soteriological whole.

depends_on.notes: Rule-of-faith prior invoked by Orthodox referee.

blocking_prior.description: Does Romans 3 control the reading of James 2 / patristic corpus, or vice-versa?

children.notes: Red-team successfully prevented silent convergence on purely forensic reading.

root.notes: Tree deliberately surfaces irreducible fork rather than forcing synthesis.

profile_outcomes.rationale_summary: Sola scriptura + historical-grammatical method isolates Rom 3:24 forensic sense as primary; imputation follows.
Trent Session 6 reads justification as both remission and renewal; forensic sense incomplete without inherent righteousness.
Forensic language acknowledged but subordinated to theosis; justification is participation in divine life, not merely declaration.

metrics.notes: Moderate stability across historical positions; high contestation due to three-tradition spread. Single-model run; provisional until cross-family audit.

version_history.note: Initial tree from Round 1 synthesis (Reformed/Catholic only).
Orthodox integration, red-team node, updated metrics and provenance.
